title: AI-Powered Productivity
source: Authored for the Salareen corporate catalog; structured to mirror the UK apprenticeship standard format.

knowledge.K1: How generative AI assistants such as Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini are embedded in everyday workplace applications.
knowledge.K2: High-value productivity use cases: drafting, summarising, rewriting, extracting action items and quick explanations.
knowledge.K3: The anatomy of an effective prompt (role, task, context, format) and techniques such as few-shot prompting and step-by-step reasoning.
knowledge.K4: How iterating on prompts improves output quality.
knowledge.K5: How grounding an assistant in your own documents reduces made-up information.
knowledge.K6: Why AI output must be reviewed and verified by a human before use.
knowledge.K7: Data-protection rules and the risks of pasting confidential, personal or regulated data into AI tools.
knowledge.K8: The difference between approved enterprise AI tools and public consumer tools.
knowledge.K9: Sources of bias in AI output and the need for transparency.
knowledge.K10: Responsible AI use and keeping humans in charge of decisions about people.
knowledge.K11: How to measure the time and quality impact of AI on tasks.
knowledge.K12: How to build reusable prompts and personal workflows that make gains stick.

skills.S1: Identify frequent, repetitive tasks where AI assistants save time.
skills.S2: Construct clear, specific prompts using role, task, context and format.
skills.S3: Apply prompting techniques such as examples, constraints and step-by-step reasoning.
skills.S4: Iterate with an assistant over several rounds to refine output.
skills.S5: Supply trusted source material to ground answers.
skills.S6: Critically review, fact-check and correct AI output before sharing.
skills.S7: Apply data-protection policy and use only approved tools for sensitive data.
skills.S8: Detect bias or errors and handle them responsibly.
skills.S9: Document and reuse effective prompts in a personal library.
skills.S10: Measure the before-and-after impact of AI on a task.
skills.S11: Be transparent about AI use and maintain human oversight.
skills.S12: Share effective techniques with colleagues to spread productivity gains.

behaviours.B1: Self-directed in learning new AI tools and techniques.
behaviours.B2: Takes responsibility for the accuracy and quality of AI-assisted work.
behaviours.B3: Collaborative and generous in sharing knowledge.
behaviours.B4: Acts with integrity regarding data, honesty and AI use.
behaviours.B5: Adapts confidently as AI tools change.

Duty 1.statement: Apply AI assistants to everyday productivity tasks.

Duty 2.statement: Prompt and iterate effectively to produce strong drafts.

Duty 3.statement: Ground answers and verify AI output before use.

Duty 4.statement: Protect data and use approved tools in line with policy.

Duty 5.statement: Use AI responsibly, transparently and without causing bias harm.

Duty 6.statement: Build reusable workflows and measure and share impact.
